messages.content: You are AEGIS Build Agent. Your first action MUST be a tool call to list_files to inspect the project structure. Do not output text or JSON content on the first turn.
Inspect the project at /home/kosh/AEGIS/uploads/proj-probe to begin building it.

function.name: list_files
function.description: 프로젝트 디렉토리 구조를 트리 형태로 반환한다. 전체 구조를 파악할 때 가장 먼저 사용하라.

path.description: (선택) 특정 하위 디렉토리만 탐색. 기본: 프로젝트 루트

function.name: read_file
function.description: 프로젝트 내 파일을 읽는다 (읽기 전용, 8KB 제한).

function.name: write_file
function.description: build-aegis-4c1d52499d913fa8/ 폴더 안에 파일을 생성한다 (빌드 스크립트, toolchain 파일 등).

function.name: edit_file
function.description: build-aegis-4c1d52499d913fa8/ 내 에이전트가 생성한 파일을 수정한다 (전체 덮어쓰기).

function.name: delete_file
function.description: build-aegis-4c1d52499d913fa8/ 내 에이전트가 생성한 파일을 삭제한다.

function.name: try_build
function.description: S4에 빌드 명령어를 전송하여 실행한다. exitCode=0이면 성공.

build_environment.description: (선택) caller가 제공한 명시적 환경변수 주입. 값은 문자열이어야 한다.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to inspect the project at `/home/kosh/AEGIS/uploads/proj-probe` to begin building it. I must first use the `list_files` tool to get the project structure. Since the user provided an absolute path, I'll use it as the `path` parameter, though the tool description mentions it expects a relative path from the project root. However, usually, if a specific path is provided, I should try to list files from there or just list the root if the provided path is the context. Let's look at the tool definition again: `path`: "(선택) 특정 하위 디렉토리만 탐색. 기본: 프로젝트 루트". The user gave `/home/kosh/AEGIS/uploads/proj-probe`. I will try to list files. Since I can't pass absolute paths if the tool expects relative ones, but the prompt implies I'm working on this project. I'll start by listing the files. Wait, the system prompt says "Your first action MUST be a tool call to list_files to inspect the project structure." I will call `list_files`. I will assume the current working context is the project root, so I might not need to pass the absolute path, or I can pass it if the tool supports it. Let's just call `list_files` without a path first to see the root, or pass the path if I can. Given the phrasing "Inspect the project at /home/...", the current context is likely that directory. I'll call `list_files` with no path to get the root structure, as that's the standard first step.

Actually, looking at previous examples or standard behavior for these agents, if a path is given, it's often the root. I'll just call `list_files` with `max_depth=3` to get a good overview. I won't pass the absolute path unless necessary, as the tool usually operates on the mounted project.

Let's call `list_files`.
